goal: Implement ITRP Application — Invoice Trading & Receivables Program for Randstad FTC. FastAPI backend + Next.js frontend + GCP (Firestore, BigQuery, Cloud Run). Replace legacy CREST system. Go-live: 30 June 2026.

tasks.name: verify-postgresql-firestore-replacement
tasks.description: Verify that all PostgreSQL/Cloud SQL references in documentation were correctly replaced with Firestore. Check each of 14 files. Fix any missed or incorrect replacements.
tasks.instruction:
1. Grep for remaining PostgreSQL/Postgres/Cloud SQL references in track_changes/.documentation/
2. For each remaining hit: read context, replace with Firestore
3. Check ARC-FINAL-ITRP-application.md most carefully — it's the architecture doc
4. Verify the architecture diagram text shows Firestore, not Cloud SQL
5. Check imp_plan.md tasks — any that reference PostgreSQL migrations, SQL schemas, Cloud SQL setup must be updated
6. Check DECISIONS.md — ADR entries about Cloud SQL should be updated to reflect Firestore decision
7. Check NFR-001 — performance requirements referencing PostgreSQL response times
8. Verify no broken references (e.g. 'Firestore row-level lock' which doesn't exist)
acceptance_criteria: Zero grep hits for PostgreSQL, Postgres, Cloud SQL in .documentation/
ARC-FINAL architecture diagram shows Firestore
No references to SQL-specific features (row-level locks, SQL migrations) without Firestore equivalent noted

tasks.name: create-discrepancies-document
tasks.description: Create DISCREPANCIES.md documenting all contradictions, unclear items, and things that need client clarification. Sources: documentation vs code, documentation vs documentation, open items from analysis files.
tasks.instruction:
Create track_changes/.documentation/DISCREPANCIES.md with sections:

1. ARCHITECTURE DISCREPANCIES
- Original ARC-FINAL said PostgreSQL, code uses Firestore (RESOLVED: Firestore stays)
- Original ARC-FINAL said direct BigQuery cross-project reads from Warsaw, actual: own temp_invoice table (RESOLVED)
- Original ARC-FINAL said PG->BQ nightly sync, actual: dual-write at purchase time (RESOLVED)
- ARC-FINAL mentions Redis elimination but code never had Redis
- ARC-FINAL mentions Next.js SSR but current code uses client-side rendering with useSearchParams

2. BUSINESS RULES UNCLEAR
- Credit limit: source (Warsaw or manual?), threshold enforcement (pause vs reject?)
- Mid Office role: exact permissions undefined ('limited' not specified)
- Week boundary: UTC or country-local time? (TODO.md item 1.1)
- File completion gate: mechanism undefined (TODO.md item S.1)
- Step 0 data quality: validation rules TBD
- sales_type field vs sell_invoice boolean (TODO.md item 2.1)
- intercompany field confirmation needed (TODO.md item 2.2)
- Priority expiry after run: clear or persist? (TODO.md item 3.3)

3. DOCUMENTATION vs CODE GAPS
- Dashboard: doc describes status overview per country, code has 7-step orchestration runner
- ITRP main: doc describes full financial picture with cashflow, code has simple preview+execute
- Settings: doc describes start dates + auto-load + auto-buy schedules, code has only auto-buy schedule
- Maintenance: doc describes Load Data + Backup Restore, code has placeholder tabs
- Reporting: doc describes 4 report types, code has stub endpoint
- My Customers: doc describes customer management, code has nothing

4. OPEN ITEMS FROM ANALYSIS FILES
- Read each analysis file (AUTO-BUY, CREDIT-LIMIT, SCHEDULER, etc) and extract unresolved items

5. DEPENDENCIES ON EXTERNAL TEAMS
- Warsaw BigQuery schema contract (DEP-005)
- Apigee setup
- GCP access (reported 50%)
- Business rules finalization (20% incomplete, deadline passed)
- Mockup approval status unknown
acceptance_criteria: DISCREPANCIES.md exists with all 5 sections
Each discrepancy has status: RESOLVED, OPEN, or NEEDS_CLARIFICATION
Open items from all analysis files included

tasks.name: reorganize-documentation-structure
tasks.description: Reorganize .documentation/ into clear categories. Create an INDEX.md that maps every document to its purpose and status.
tasks.instruction:
Create track_changes/.documentation/INDEX.md with:

## Documentation Index

### Architecture & Design
- ARC-FINAL-ITRP-application.md — Final architecture (Firestore + BigQuery, modular monolith)
- VIS-001-ITRP-application.md — Visual design specifications
- DECISIONS.md — Architecture Decision Records

### Requirements
- REQ-001-ITRP-application.md — Functional requirements
- NFR-001-ITRP-application.md — Non-functional requirements
- DEP-001-ITRP-application.md — Dependencies and external teams

### Implementation
- project.md — Full technical specification (MASTER REFERENCE)
- imp_plan.md — Implementation plan with phases and tasks
- ITRP_Knowledge_Base.md — Domain knowledge base

### Page Specifications (UI)
- pages/01-dashboard.md through pages/08-user-flow.md

### Analysis Documents
- AUTO-BUY-analysis.md, CREDIT-LIMIT-CHECK-analysis.md, etc.
- SPEC-auto-buy-schedule-maintenance.md

### Project Management
- Meeting-notes/ — Meeting records
- DISCREPANCIES.md — Open issues and contradictions

For each document: one line with title, what it covers, and whether it's CURRENT/OUTDATED/NEEDS-UPDATE.

Also review project.md (the master spec) for any remaining PostgreSQL references and fix them.
acceptance_criteria: INDEX.md exists with categorized list of all documents
Each document has status: CURRENT, NEEDS-UPDATE, or OUTDATED
project.md has zero PostgreSQL references

tasks.name: align-imp-plan-with-reality
tasks.description: Update imp_plan.md to reflect actual code state. Mark completed tasks, adjust task descriptions to match Firestore reality, add missing tasks discovered from code analysis.
tasks.instruction:
Read imp_plan.md fully. For each TASK-XXX:
1. Check if the task's deliverable already exists in code (use code analysis from K-001)
2. If DONE in code: mark as DONE with note what implements it
3. If PARTIAL: note what's done and what remains
4. If task references PostgreSQL: update to Firestore equivalent
5. If task is blocked by external dependency: note the blocker

Specific updates needed:
- TASK-001 (GCP Setup): PARTIAL — Firestore and BQ exist, Cloud Run TBD
- TASK-003 (Auth): DONE — Mock SSO implemented
- TASK-005 (Firestore Setup): DONE — seed data creates 13 collections
- TASK-301 (RBAC): DONE — full CRUD with transactions
- TASK-302 (Audit): DONE — immutable logging
- TASK-401 (Buying Service): PARTIAL — steps 1-7 exist but with gaps (see TODO.md)
- Tasks referencing 'PostgreSQL schema' or 'SQL migrations': rewrite for Firestore

Also add any tasks that are in the code but NOT in the plan (e.g. scheduler trigger endpoint).
acceptance_criteria: Every TASK in imp_plan.md has status: DONE, PARTIAL, TODO, or BLOCKED
Zero PostgreSQL references in imp_plan.md
Missing tasks from code identified

tasks.name: customers-backend-schemas
tasks.description: Create Pydantic schemas for customers module: CustomerSummary, CustomerDetail, CustomerExclusion, CustomerListResponse.
tasks.instruction:
Create ITRP/backend/app/schemas/customers.py following the pattern in schemas/autobuy.py.

Schemas needed:
1. CustomerSummary: customer_id (str), customer_name (str), open_amount (Decimal), currency (str), invoice_count (int), country (str)
2. CustomerDetail: extends CustomerSummary with: legal_entity_code (str), credit_limit (Decimal|None), current_exposure (Decimal), is_excluded (bool), exclusion_reason (str|None), sales_type_override (str|None)
3. CustomerExclusionRequest: customer_id (str), reason (str)
4. CustomerExclusionResponse: customer_id (str), customer_name (str), excluded_by (str), excluded_at (datetime), reason (str)
5. CustomerListResponse: items (list[CustomerSummary]), total (int), tab (str: 'active'|'all'|'previously_purchased')

Follow existing patterns: use Field() for validation, Decimal for amounts.
acceptance_criteria: schemas/customers.py exists with all 5 schema classes
Schemas use Pydantic BaseModel with proper types
Decimal used for monetary amounts

produces.schemas: CustomerSummary, CustomerDetail, CustomerExclusionRequest, CustomerExclusionResponse, CustomerListResponse in schemas/customers.py

tasks.name: customers-firestore-collection
tasks.description: Add CUSTOMER_EXCLUSIONS collection to collections.py. Create customers service with Firestore operations for exclusions.
tasks.instruction:
1. Add to ITRP/backend/app/collections.py:
   CUSTOMER_EXCLUSIONS = 'customer_exclusions'

2. Create ITRP/backend/app/modules/customers/__init__.py (empty)

3. Create ITRP/backend/app/modules/customers/service.py with functions:
   - async def get_customer_exclusions(db, country: str) -> dict[str, dict]:
     Read all docs from CUSTOMER_EXCLUSIONS where country == country. Return {customer_id: {reason, excluded_by, excluded_at}}
   - async def exclude_customer(db, customer_id: str, country: str, reason: str, user_email: str) -> dict:
     Write to CUSTOMER_EXCLUSIONS collection. Doc ID: f'{country}_{customer_id}'. Include audit trail entry.
   - async def include_customer(db, customer_id: str, country: str, user_email: str) -> None:
     Delete from CUSTOMER_EXCLUSIONS. Include audit trail entry.

Follow patterns from modules/schedule/repository.py for Firestore operations.
Follow patterns from modules/audit/service.py or audit/router.py for audit trail recording.
Use app.firestore.get_db() for database client.
acceptance_criteria: CUSTOMER_EXCLUSIONS constant in collections.py
customers/service.py has get_customer_exclusions, exclude_customer, include_customer
All mutations write audit trail entries

produces.collection: customer_exclusions in Firestore with docs: {customer_id, country, reason, excluded_by, excluded_at}

tasks.name: customers-backend-router
tasks.description: Create customers router with endpoints: list customers (from BQ), get customer detail, exclude/include customer. Register in main.py.
tasks.instruction:
1. Create ITRP/backend/app/modules/customers/router.py with:

   router = APIRouter(prefix='/api/customers', tags=['customers'])

   GET /api/customers?country={code}&tab={active|all}&search={text}&page={n}&page_size={n}&sort_by={field}&sort_order={asc|desc}
   - tab=active: customers with open_amount > 0 in current week's invoices
   - tab=all: all distinct customers from temp_invoice
   - Query BigQuery temp_invoice: SELECT DISTINCT customer_id, customer_invoice_name, customer_billing_country_code, SUM(open_invoice_amount) as open_amount, COUNT(*) as invoice_count FROM temp_invoice WHERE customer_billing_country_code = @country GROUP BY customer_id, customer_invoice_name, customer_billing_country_code
   - For 'active': add WHERE open_invoice_amount > 0
   - Merge with exclusions from Firestore (is_excluded flag)
   - Return CustomerListResponse
   - Requires permission: purchase:read or reporting:read

   GET /api/customers/{customer_id}?country={code}
   - Detail view: invoices for this customer from BQ + credit limit info + exclusion status
   - Return CustomerDetail

   POST /api/customers/{customer_id}/exclude
   - Body: CustomerExclusionRequest
   - Requires permission: purchase:execute
   - Calls exclude_customer() from service
   - Return CustomerExclusionResponse

   DELETE /api/customers/{customer_id}/exclude?country={code}
   - Requires permission: purchase:execute
   - Calls include_customer() from service
   - Return 204

   GET /api/customers/excluded?country={code}
   - List all excluded customers for a country
   - Reads from Firestore CUSTOMER_EXCLUSIONS

2. Register in main.py:
   from app.modules.customers.router import router as customers_router
   app.include_router(customers_router)

Follow patterns from modules/buying/router.py for BigQuery queries.
Follow patterns from modules/rbac/router.py for permission checks (require_permission).
Use from app.auth.dependencies import get_current_user, require_permission.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/customers returns customer list from BigQuery with exclusion status
GET /api/customers/{id} returns customer detail with invoices
POST /api/customers/{id}/exclude creates exclusion in Firestore with audit trail
DELETE /api/customers/{id}/exclude removes exclusion with audit trail
Router registered in main.py
All endpoints check RBAC permissions

tasks.name: customers-frontend-page
tasks.description: Create My Customers page with 3 sub-tabs (Active, All, Previously Purchased), customer table with search/sort, and exclude action.
tasks.instruction:
Create ITRP/frontend/src/app/customers/page.tsx

Follow patterns from admin/page.tsx (tab structure, table, modals).
Use Ant Design components: Tabs, Table, Input.Search, Button, Tag, Modal.
Use TanStack Query for data fetching (pattern from dashboard/page.tsx).

Page structure:
1. Three Tabs: 'Active customers ({count})' | 'All customers ({count})' | 'Previously Purchased'
2. Search bar above table
3. Table columns:
   - Customer/Group ID (blue link, clickable → opens detail modal)
   - Customer name (link)
   - Open amount (formatted with currency)
   - Actions: Eye icon (detail preview), Exclude button (if has permission)
4. Detail modal (Ant Design Drawer or Modal):
   - Customer info header
   - Invoice list for this customer
   - Credit limit info (if available)
   - Exclude/Include button

API calls:
- GET /api/backend/api/customers?country={selected}&tab={tab}&search={text}
- POST /api/backend/api/customers/{id}/exclude (body: {customer_id, reason})
- DELETE /api/backend/api/customers/{id}/exclude?country={code}

Permission checks:
- Use PermissionGate from components/auth/PermissionGate.tsx
- Exclude button: only show if hasPermission('purchase', 'execute')
- Page access: require hasPermission('purchase', 'read') or hasPermission('reporting', 'read')

Country selector:
- Reuse the country fetching pattern from itrp/page.tsx (GET /api/buying/countries)
- Default to first country or user's scoped country

Update navigation in components/layout/AppLayout.tsx:
- 'My Customers' nav item should link to /customers (it already exists in nav but goes nowhere)
acceptance_criteria: /customers page renders with 3 tabs
Customer table shows data from backend API
Search filters table by customer name or ID
Eye icon opens detail view with customer invoices
Exclude button creates exclusion (with reason input)
Include button removes exclusion
Permission gates hide exclude button for read-only roles
Navigation links to /customers correctly
exclusions: DO NOT modify other pages except AppLayout nav link

tasks.name: customers-integration-test
tasks.description: Verify full customer flow end-to-end: list → detail → exclude → verify exclusion shows in list → include → verify removed.
tasks.instruction:
Manual verification checklist:
1. Start containers: docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml up
2. Login as admin@randstad.com
3. Navigate to /customers
4. Verify Active customers tab shows customers from BQ
5. Verify All customers tab shows more customers
6. Search for a customer name — verify filter works
7. Click eye icon — verify detail modal opens with invoice list
8. Click Exclude on a customer — enter reason — verify exclusion saved
9. Refresh page — verify excluded customer shows is_excluded tag
10. Click Include to remove exclusion — verify tag removed
11. Login as opco.pl@randstad.com — verify only PL customers visible
12. Login as opco.pl — verify Exclude button not visible (no purchase:execute permission)

If any step fails: create a bug task in Forge pipeline.
acceptance_criteria: Login as admin succeeds and returns valid JWT
GET /api/customers?country=PL returns items list with total
GET /api/customers/{id}?country=PL returns customer detail
POST exclude -> verify is_excluded=true -> DELETE include -> verify is_excluded removed
OpCo PL user sees PL customers, NL returns 403 Forbidden
Audit trail contains EXCLUDE_CUSTOMER entries after exclusion

tasks.name: customers-country-scoping
tasks.description: OpCo user with scope_country=PL can currently query /api/customers?country=DE and see data they should not access. Fix: pass country to RBAC permission check.
tasks.instruction: In backend/app/modules/customers/router.py, after getting the user from require_any_permission, check if user has country-scoped permissions and if so, verify the requested country matches. Either modify require_any_permission to accept country param, or add manual check after the dependency returns the user. Read auth/dependencies.py _has_permission — it already supports country param, but require_any_permission does not pass it through.
acceptance_criteria: User with scope_country=PL requesting country=DE gets 403 Forbidden
User with global scope (no scope_country) can query any country

tasks.name: customers-previously-purchased-tab
tasks.description: Add Previously Purchased sub-tab to My Customers page. Shows purchase history per customer from BigQuery purchase_records table.
tasks.instruction: Backend: add GET /api/customers/purchased?country={code} endpoint that queries BigQuery purchase_records table grouped by customer. Frontend: add third tab to customers/page.tsx. Mock data fallback needed.

tasks.name: customers-sales-type-override

tasks.name: integrate-exclusions-into-buying-eligibility
tasks.description: Add eligibility rule to buying service: check customer_exclusions Firestore collection and exclude invoices from excluded customers.
tasks.instruction: In buying/service.py step_2_eligibility_filters: after existing 7 rules, add Rule 8 — read customer_exclusions for the country, exclude all invoices where customer_id is in exclusions dict. Log excluded invoices with rule_name=customer_excluded.

Exclusion appears in step 2 results with rule_name customer_excluded

4. Verify seed data by reading opcos collection after restart

tasks.name: expand-opco-seed-to-75
tasks.description: Expand OpCo seed data from 12 to ~75 records covering all 11 countries with realistic names and Tagetik codes.
tasks.instruction:
1. In seed.py OPCOS list: add OpCos for missing countries (FR, PT, CA, IT, AU) and more for existing countries
2. Target distribution: PL: 8, DE: 7, NL: 6, FR: 6, IT: 6, SE: 5, AU: 5, CA: 5, US: 5, BE: 4, PT: 3 = ~60 total (realistic)
3. Use realistic names like 'Randstad France SAS', 'Randstad Italia SpA' etc
4. Mix of statuses: most On, a few Off (test Rule 4), one New per country (test Rule 5)
5. All with autobuy_enabled: True except Off ones
6. start_date: '2025-01-01' for most, '2026-03-18' for a few (test Rule 3)
7. Also create opco_schedules entries for any new countries not yet seeded
acceptance_criteria: OPCOS list has 60+ entries across all 11 countries
Each country has at least 3 OpCos
Mix of On/Off/New statuses for rule testing
opco_schedules covers all 11 countries

tasks.name: create-bq-schema-definitions
tasks.description: Create BigQuery table schema definitions as Python dicts or SQL files. Document what tables exist and their columns. Create seed script for BQ test data.
tasks.instruction:
1. Create my_customers/backend/bq_schemas/ directory
2. Create bq_schemas/tables.py with schema dicts for all 8 BQ tables referenced in buying/service.py: temp_invoice, purchase_records, autobuy_job_executions, autobuy_invoice_results, rpt_purchased_invoices, rpt_settlement_report_economic, rpt_settlement_report_legal
3. Each schema: list of {name, type, mode, description} matching BigQuery SchemaField format
4. If seed_bq_test_data.py already exists, verify it matches schemas
5. Ensure temp_invoice schema matches the SELECT columns in buying/service.py step_1

Schemas match the fields used in buying/service.py queries

tasks.name: verify-and-deep-verify-o001

Docker containers start without import or seed errors
All field mismatches from audit resolved or documented as decisions

tasks.name: create-customers-master-data-collection
tasks.description: Create Firestore customers collection with master data fields needed by buying service (credit_limit, default_sales_type). Add to collections.py. Create seed with realistic customer data matching BQ temp_invoice customer_ids.
tasks.instruction:
1. Add CUSTOMERS_MASTER = 'customers_master' to my_customers/backend/app/collections.py (not 'customers' to avoid confusion with customer_exclusions concept)
2. Create seed function seed_customers_master() in seed.py with ~30 customers for PL, NL, DE matching customer_ids from BQ mock data (bigquery_stub.py) and mock_data.py
3. Each customer: customer_id (str), customer_name (str), country (str), credit_limit (float, in local currency), default_sales_type (str: Economic|Legal), legal_entity_code (str, FK to OpCo), status (str: active|inactive)
4. Include realistic credit limits: range 10K-500K per customer
5. Call seed_customers_master() in seed.py main seed_all() function
acceptance_criteria: CUSTOMERS_MASTER constant in collections.py
seed_customers_master creates 30+ customer records
Customer records include credit_limit and default_sales_type fields
customer_ids match those in mock_data.py and bigquery_stub.py

tasks.name: create-sales-types-collection
tasks.description: Create Firestore sales_types collection with dynamic sales type definitions. Seed with Economic, Legal, Insurance, No.
tasks.instruction:
1. Add SALES_TYPES = 'sales_types' to collections.py
2. Create seed function seed_sales_types() with: Economic (eligible for auto-buy), Legal (eligible for auto-buy), Insurance (eligible for manual buy only), No (not eligible, collection pass-through)
3. Each sales_type: code (str), name (str), eligible_auto_buy (bool), eligible_manual_buy (bool), description (str)
4. Call in seed_all()
acceptance_criteria: SALES_TYPES constant in collections.py
4 sales types seeded: Economic, Legal, Insurance, No
Each type has eligibility flags for auto-buy and manual-buy

tasks.name: decision-legal-entities-vs-opcos
tasks.description: Document decision: legal_entities as separate collection or keep using opcos. Current code uses opco_map keyed by legal_entity_code — creating separate collection would break buying service.
tasks.instruction:
Record decision in Forge: legal_entities data is stored on opcos collection (code, name, start_date, country). A separate legal_entities collection is NOT needed because:
1. buying/service.py step_2 queries opco_map by legal_entity_code
2. OpCo code IS the legal entity code in current data model
3. project.md says 'One OpCo can have many Legal Entities' but current BQ data treats them as 1:1
4. Creating separate collection requires refactoring buying service without business value
Record as CLOSED decision.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded in Forge decisions.json
Reasoning documents why separate collection is not needed

tasks.name: verify-o002

tasks.instruction:

3. Verify sales_types match what buying service expects
4. Update O-002 KR progress

Customer IDs are consistent across seed, mock data, and BQ stub

In settings/page.tsx AutoSchedulesSection:
1. Replace the disabled toggle+pills for auto-load with an Alert component (Ant Design)

Message explains why it's not available

tasks.name: settings-country-rbac-filtering
tasks.description: Filter country dropdown in Settings page based on user permissions. OpCo-scoped user should only see their country.
tasks.instruction:
In settings/page.tsx:
1. Use useAuthStore to get allowedCountries()
2. If allowedCountries() returns a set, filter COUNTRY_OPTIONS to only those

tasks.name: decision-multi-currency-schedule
tasks.description: Document decision: single schedule per country (current) vs per-currency schedule (documentation). Current code is simpler, doc spec may be over-engineered for 11 countries where 1 country = 1 currency.
tasks.instruction: Record decision in Forge: keep single schedule per country. Reasoning: 1. Each ITRP country has one primary currency, 2. Current opco_schedules stores one record per country which is sufficient, 3. Multi-currency UI adds complexity without clear business value, 4. If needed later, can extend opco_schedules with currency field.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded in Forge with reasoning

tasks.name: priority-lock-during-autobuy
tasks.description: Lock priority UI and API during active auto-buy. Check buy_locks collection before allowing priority changes.
tasks.instruction:
BACKEND (priorities/router.py):
1. In POST and DELETE endpoints, before writing: query buy_locks collection for active lock on requested country.
2. If lock exists and not expired: return HTTP 409 Conflict with message 'Auto-buy in progress for {country}. Priority changes locked.'
3. Add GET /api/priorities/lock-status?country=XX endpoint returning {locked: bool, locked_since: timestamp|null}.

FRONTEND (priorities/page.tsx):
4. Query lock-status on page load and on interval (every 10s).
5. When locked: disable Add Priority button, disable Delete buttons, disable Up/Down buttons, show Alert banner 'Priorities locked - auto-buy in progress for {country}'.
6. When unlocked: normal behavior.

TESTS:
7. Test POST returns 409 when lock active.
8. Test DELETE returns 409 when lock active.
9. Test lock-status endpoint.

acceptance_criteria.text: POST /api/priorities returns 409 when buy_lock active for country
DELETE /api/priorities/{id} returns 409 when buy_lock active
GET /api/priorities/lock-status returns correct lock state
Frontend disables all edit controls and shows lock banner during auto-buy

tasks.name: frontend-route-guards-all-pages

   - ITRP/Maintenance: maintenance:read

   - Manual Buy: purchase:execute

4. Test by logging in as opco user - should see 403 on Settings, Maintenance, Priorities, Manual Buy.
